matter.title: José-María Düböis O'Callaghan-Núñez Jr. v. Ändersson, Fitzwilliam & Sønsteby Timber Holdings, LLC
matter.summary: José-María Düböis O'Callaghan-Núñez Jr. sues Ändersson, Fitzwilliam & Sønsteby Timber Holdings, LLC for breach of a delivered-sale contract for 500 cords of seasoned firewood worth $69405, after only 141 cords were delivered by 2025-10-09.

defendant.organization_name: Ändersson, Fitzwilliam & Sønsteby Timber Holdings, LLC
defendant.full_name: Ändersson, Fitzwilliam & Sønsteby Timber Holdings, LLC

fact_pattern.summary: José-María Düböis O'Callaghan-Núñez Jr. sues Ändersson, Fitzwilliam & Sønsteby Timber Holdings, LLC for breach of a delivered-sale contract for 500 cords of seasoned firewood worth $69405, after only 141 cords were delivered by 2025-10-09.
fact_pattern.narrative:
José-María Düböis O'Callaghan-Núñez Jr. contracted with Ändersson, Fitzwilliam & Sønsteby Timber Holdings, LLC for a delivered-sale contract for 500 cords of seasoned firewood at a total price of $69405, delivery due by 2025-10-09. Only 141 cords arrived.

The seller invokes paragraph 14 — the “force majeure” clause — citing an “unusually wet harvest season” — but the clause requires written notice within ten days, which was never given. A demand letter went out 2025-10-30 and drew no substantive response.

Counsel Olivia B. Ellsworth of Kingsley & Associates will plead breach of contract and seek cover damages for replacement wood purchased on the spot market.
undisputed_facts: The parties signed the contract for $69405.
Only 141 of 500 cords were delivered by 2025-10-09.
disputed_facts: Whether paragraph 14 — the “force majeure” clause excuses the shortfall without the required written notice.
The plaintiff's cover damages and incidental costs.

exchanges.question: What did you agree to?
exchanges.answer: a delivered-sale contract for 500 cords of seasoned firewood, $69405 total, delivered by 2025-10-09.

exchanges.question: What actually happened?
exchanges.answer: They delivered 141 cords and went quiet — no notice under paragraph 14 — the “force majeure” clause, nothing.

exchanges.question: What did the shortfall cost you?
exchanges.answer: I had to buy replacement wood at spot prices and lost two resale customers.

sub_questions: Were the spot purchases commercially reasonable?

issues.issue: Force majeure as an affirmative defense
sub_questions: Was the required ten-day written notice given?

amounts.label: Cover purchases
amounts.basis: Replacement wood bought on the spot market.

facts.contract_description: a delivered-sale contract for 500 cords of seasoned firewood
